questions.question: Refer to the Bell 206L-1 Long Ranger II Performance and Operations Handbook. With a Pressure Altitude of 2,500ft, an OAT of +16˚C and a gross weight of 1,647kg. What is the maximum ROC using Max Continuous Power with all door on?

questions.question: Which of the following applies to a basic HLS?
options: can be used for day and night operations can be used for day operations only
requires a safety area extending 3m around the FATO
capabile of enclosing a circle of 1.5 times the 'D' value of the largest helicopter to be operated.

questions.question: What does the D value of a helicopter refer to?
options: the maximum dimensions of the helicopter
the largest radius of the helicopters rotor disc
the maximum operating weight of the helicopter

questions.question:
Find the CP/ETP given:
Point A to Point B = 124nm
TAS=95kts
with an outbound headwind component of 14kts
questions.explanation:
95 - 14 = 81kts outbound
95 + 14 = 109kts inbound
ETP = (124nm x 109kts)/(109kts+81kts) = 71.1nm
questions.kd: Unit 1.9.5   5.3.2 Calculate time and distance to an ETP or PNR between 2 points, using planned or given data

questions.question:
Find the CP/ETP given:
Point A to Point B = 157nm
TAS=83kts
with an outbound headwind component of 16kts and a tailwind component inbound of 12kts
questions.explanation:
83- 16 = 67kts outbound
83 + 12 = 95kts inbound
ETP = (157nm x 95kts)/(95kts+67kts) = 92.06nm
questions.kd: Unit 1.9.5   5.3.2 Calculate time and distance to an ETP or PNR between 2 points, using planned or given data

questions.question:
Find the CP/ETP given:
Point A to Point B = 103nm
TAS=105kts
with an outbound tailwind component of 18kts and a inbound headwind component of 14kts
questions.explanation:
103 + 18 = 121kts outbound
103 - 14 = 89kts inbound
ETP = (105nm x 121kts)/(121kts+89kts) = 60.5nm
questions.kd: Unit 1.9.5   5.3.2 Calculate time and distance to an ETP or PNR between 2 points, using planned or given data

questions.question:
Find the CP/ETP given:
Point A to Point B = 186nm
TAS=110kts
with an outbound tailwind component of 22kts and an inbound headwind component of 6kts
questions.explanation:
110 + 22 = 132kts outbound
110 - 6 = 114kts inbound
ETP = (186nm x 114kts)/(132kts+114kts) = 86.2nm
questions.kd: Unit 1.9.5   5.3.2 Calculate time and distance to an ETP or PNR between 2 points, using planned or given data

questions.question:
Find the CP/ETP given:
Point A to Point B = 86nm
TAS=75kts
with an outbound headwind component of 14kts and an inbound headwind component of 5kts
questions.explanation:
75 - 14 = 61kts outbound
75 - 5 = 70kts inbound
ETP = (83nm x 70kts)/(70kts+61kts) = 44.4nm
questions.kd: Unit 1.9.5   5.3.2 Calculate time and distance to an ETP or PNR between 2 points, using planned or given data

questions.question: The Moment is equal to:
options: arm multiplied by the station weight
arm divided by the station weight
questions.kd: Unit 1.9.7   1.6.1(c) moment

questions.question:
Find the PNR in nautical miles given: 
Fuel onboard at start up: 118minutes
Fixed Reserve: 20 minutes
Contingency Fuel: 10%
Start/Taxi Fuel: 5 minutes
TAS = 75kts
Wind: Outbound = 12kt headwind, inbound 14kt tailwind
questions.explanation:
75 - 12 = 63kt outbound
75+14 = 89kts inbound
TSE = (118-5-20)/1.1 = 84.5 minutes
PNR (mins) = 84.5 x 89/63+89 = 49 minutes
PNR (nm) = 49mins @ 63kts = 51.5nm
questions.kd: Unit 1.9.5   5.3.2 Calculate time and distance to an ETP or PNR between 2 points, using planned or given data

questions.question:
Find the PNR in nautical miles given: 
Fuel onboard at start up: 156minutes
Fixed Reserve: 20 minutes
Contingency Fuel: 10%
Start/Taxi Fuel: 5 minutes
TAS = 105kts
Wind: Outhead = 12kt tailwind, inbound 14kt headwind
questions.explanation:
105 + 12 = 117kt outbound
105-14 = 91kts inbound
TSE = (156-5-20)/1.1 = 119.1 minutes
PNR (mins) = 119.1 x 91/117+91 = 52 minutes
PNR (nm) = 52mins @ 117kts = 101.4nm
questions.kd: Unit 1.9.5   5.3.2 Calculate time and distance to an ETP or PNR between 2 points, using planned or given data

questions.question:
Find the PNR from the departure point in nautical miles given: 
Fuel onboard at start up: 123 minutes
Fixed Reserve: 20 minutes
Contingency Fuel: 10%
Start/Taxi Fuel: 5 minutes
TAS = 90kts
Wind: Outbound = 22kt tailwind, inbound 19kt headwind
questions.explanation:
90 + 22 = 112kt outbound
90-19 = 71kts inbound
TSE = (123-5-20)/1.1 = 89.1 minutes
PNR (mins) = 89.1 x 71/112+71 = 34.6minutes
PNR (nm) = 34.6mins @ 112kts = 64.5nm
questions.kd: Unit 1.9.5   5.3.2 Calculate time and distance to an ETP or PNR between 2 points, using planned or given data

questions.question:
Find the PNR in nautical miles given: 
Fuel onboard at start up: 91 minutes
Fixed Reserve: 20 minutes
Contingency Fuel: 10%
Start/Taxi Fuel: 5 minutes
TAS = 85kts
Wind: Outhead = 10kt tailwind, inbound 8kt headwind
questions.explanation:
85 + 10= 95kt outbound
86-8 = 78kts inbound
TSE = (91-5-20)/1.1 = 60 minutes
PNR (mins) = 60 x 78/95+78 = 27 minutes
PNR (nm) = 27mins @ 95kts = 42.8nm
questions.kd: Unit 1.9.5   5.3.2 Calculate time and distance to an ETP or PNR between 2 points, using planned or given data

questions.question:
Find the PNR in nautical miles given: 
Fuel onboard at start up: 160 minutes
Fixed Reserve: 20 minutes
Contingency Fuel: 10%
Start/Taxi Fuel: 5 minutes
TAS = 110kts
Wind: Outbound= 22kt headwind, inbound 15kt tailwind
questions.explanation:
110- 22= 88kt outbound
86+15 =101kts inbound
1TSE = (160-5-20)/1.1 = 122.7 minutes
PNR (mins) = 122.7 x 101/88+101 = 65.6 minutes
PNR (nm) = 65.6mins @ 88kts = 96.2nm
questions.kd: Unit 1.9.5   5.3.2 Calculate time and distance to an ETP or PNR between 2 points, using planned or given data

questions.question: How long before a flight should you submit a flight plan?
options: 15 minutes before entry into controlled airspace
30 minutes before entry into controlled airspace
60 minutes before entry into controlled airspace
120 minutes before entry into controlled airspace
